meta.description: Curated US sky-watching spots: dark-sky places, national parks, and iconic viewpoints.
meta.coordinates_note: Coordinates are approximate centroids/access points and are good enough for distance ranking and rough drive times. Refine for production.
meta.bortle_note: bortle values are ESTIMATES (1 = darkest sky, 9 = inner-city). Replace with a real VIIRS / lightpollutionmap lookup when ready.

spots.name: Rattlesnake Ledge
spots.notes: Popular short hike near North Bend; open western view, treeline to the east.

spots.name: Mount Rainier — Sunrise Point
spots.notes: High-elevation point; very dark. Road typically closed in winter.

spots.name: Mount Rainier — Paradise
spots.notes: Open southern sky in summer; check road access.

spots.name: North Cascades National Park
spots.notes: Remote and dark; mountain horizons block low views.

spots.name: Hurricane Ridge (Olympic NP)
spots.notes: Open ridge with wide horizons.

spots.name: Crater Lake National Park
spots.notes: Designated dark sky; very clear high-desert air.

spots.name: Smith Rock State Park
spots.notes: High desert, open southern sky.

spots.name: Glacier Point (Yosemite)
spots.notes: Iconic overlook; seasonal road.

spots.name: Death Valley National Park
spots.notes: Gold-tier dark sky; some of the darkest skies in the US.

spots.name: Joshua Tree National Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park; glow from Coachella Valley to the west.

spots.name: Lassen Volcanic National Park
spots.notes: Remote, very dark.

spots.name: Pinnacles National Park
spots.notes: Reasonably dark within reach of the Bay Area.

spots.name: Mount Tamalpais
spots.notes: Bay Area glow; great sunsets over the Pacific.

spots.name: Grand Canyon National Park (South Rim)
spots.notes: Certified dark sky park.

spots.name: Flagstaff (Buffalo Park)
spots.notes: World's first Dark Sky City; town glow but well-managed.

spots.name: Sedona (Airport Mesa)
spots.notes: Dark sky community; red-rock sunsets.

spots.name: Saguaro National Park
spots.notes: Tucson glow to the east; good western sunsets.

spots.name: Bryce Canyon National Park
spots.notes: Among the darkest skies in the country.

spots.name: Arches National Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park; Moab glow to the south.

spots.name: Canyonlands National Park (Island in the Sky)
spots.notes: Very dark; wide open horizons.

spots.name: Zion National Park
spots.notes: Canyon walls block low horizons.

spots.name: Capitol Reef National Park
spots.notes: Gold-tier dark sky.

spots.name: Great Basin National Park
spots.notes: Some of the darkest skies in the lower 48.

spots.name: Rocky Mountain National Park
spots.notes: High peaks block low views; Denver glow to the SE.

spots.name: Great Sand Dunes National Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park; open southern horizon.

spots.name: Black Canyon of the Gunnison
spots.notes: Dark sky park.

spots.name: Big Bend National Park
spots.notes: Darkest skies in the contiguous US.

spots.name: Enchanted Rock
spots.notes: Dark sky area in the Hill Country.

spots.name: Guadalupe Mountains National Park
spots.notes: Remote and dark.

spots.name: Cherry Springs State Park
spots.notes: Premier East Coast dark sky park; open 360° field.

spots.name: Cadillac Mountain (Acadia)
spots.notes: First place to see sunrise in the US for part of the year.

spots.name: Katahdin Woods and Waters
spots.notes: Designated dark sky; very remote.

spots.name: Shenandoah National Park (Big Meadows)
spots.notes: Ridge-top meadows; DC/Richmond glow on horizons.

spots.name: Great Smoky Mountains (Clingmans Dome)
spots.notes: Highest point in the park; often cloudy/humid.

spots.name: Mauna Kea Visitor Station
spots.notes: World-class astronomy site; altitude caution.

spots.name: Haleakala Summit
spots.notes: Famous sunrise; reservation required for sunrise.

spots.name: Denali National Park
spots.notes: No true darkness near summer solstice; great in shoulder seasons.

spots.name: Glacier National Park (Logan Pass)
spots.notes: Dark sky park; high mountain horizons.

spots.name: Yellowstone (Lamar Valley)
spots.notes: Very dark; open valley.

spots.name: Grand Teton (Schwabacher Landing)
spots.notes: Tetons block western horizon; superb to the east.

spots.name: Craters of the Moon
spots.notes: Dark sky monument; open lava-plain horizons.

spots.name: Sawtooth (Redfish Lake)
spots.notes: Central Idaho Dark Sky Reserve.

spots.name: Badlands National Park
spots.notes: Open prairie horizons; very dark.

spots.name: Theodore Roosevelt National Park
spots.notes: Remote and dark.

spots.name: Voyageurs National Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park; northern lights possible.

spots.name: Headlands Dark Sky Park
spots.notes: Lake Michigan shoreline; open northern view.

spots.name: White Sands National Park
spots.notes: Open dunes; missile-range closures occasionally.

spots.name: Chaco Culture (Dark Sky Park)
spots.notes: Gold-tier dark sky; rough access road.

spots.name: Everglades National Park
spots.notes: Dark for the SE US; humid, buggy.

spots.name: Kissimmee Prairie Preserve
spots.notes: Florida's darkest designated sky.

spots.name: Assateague Island
spots.notes: Atlantic horizon; coastal glow north and south.

spots.name: Mount Washington
spots.notes: Extreme weather; access seasonal.

spots.name: Sand Beach (Acadia)
spots.notes: Ocean sunrise.

spots.name: Palouse Falls State Park
spots.notes: Eastern WA; dark and open.

spots.name: Steens Mountain
spots.notes: Remote SE Oregon; extremely dark.

spots.name: Alvord Desert
spots.notes: Dry lakebed; 360° flat horizon, exceptional.

spots.name: Mono Lake (South Tufa)
spots.notes: Tufa towers; open eastern Sierra.

spots.name: Trona Pinnacles
spots.notes: Mojave; open desert horizons.

spots.name: Antelope Island State Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park near Salt Lake; lake glow.

spots.name: Dinosaur National Monument
spots.notes: Dark sky monument.

spots.name: Natural Bridges National Monument
spots.notes: World's first International Dark Sky Park.

spots.name: Cosmic Campground
spots.notes: International Dark Sky Sanctuary.

spots.name: Mojave National Preserve (Kelso Dunes)
spots.notes: Very dark; open desert.

spots.name: Anza-Borrego Desert State Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park; San Diego glow to the west.

spots.name: Mauna Loa Lookout
spots.notes: High and dark.

spots.name: Big Cypress National Preserve
spots.notes: Dark sky preserve in S Florida.

spots.name: Pictured Rocks
spots.notes: Upper Peninsula; dark Lake Superior shore.

spots.name: Wind Cave National Park
spots.notes: Open prairie; dark.

spots.name: El Morro National Monument
spots.notes: Remote NW New Mexico.

spots.name: Great Falls Park
spots.notes: Close to DC — bright sky, but a quick nature escape.

spots.name: Sandy Hook
spots.notes: NYC skyline glow; ocean sunrise.

spots.name: Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park
spots.notes: Eruption glow can affect the southern sky; check volcanic activity. Excellent overhead views.

spots.name: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park
spots.notes: Largest US national park; extremely remote and dark. Northern lights visible in winter.

spots.name: Acadia National Park
spots.notes: First International Dark Sky Park in the Eastern US. Ocean horizon to the east.

spots.name: White Mountains (Franconia Notch)
spots.notes: Open notch with low ridgeline horizons; reasonably dark for New England.

spots.name: Groton State Forest, VT
spots.notes: One of the darker spots in Vermont; lake reflections add to the view.

spots.name: Sequoia National Park
spots.notes: High Sierra; very dark. Giant trees frame views of the sky.

spots.name: Mauna Loa Observatory Road
spots.notes: Above the clouds; among the best stargazing on Earth. Cold at altitude.

spots.name: Cumberland Island National Seashore
spots.notes: Remote barrier island; no vehicle traffic, very dark for the SE coast.

spots.name: Congaree National Park
spots.notes: Dark sky park; swampy lowland with open canopy.

spots.name: Little Bighorn (Big Sky Country)
spots.notes: Open eastern Montana plains; extremely dark and flat 360° horizon.

spots.name: Boundary Waters Canoe Area
spots.notes: Dark sky wilderness; northern lights frequent in fall/winter.

spots.name: Tallgrass Prairie NP, KS
spots.notes: Open prairie; very flat horizon in all directions.

spots.name: Ouachita National Forest, AR
spots.notes: Reasonably dark for the South; rolling forested hills.

spots.name: Natchez Trace Parkway
spots.notes: Rural corridor; darker than most of Mississippi.

spots.name: Dolly Sods Wilderness, WV
spots.notes: High plateau; one of the darkest spots in the Mid-Atlantic.

spots.name: Dauphin Island (Gulf Coast)
spots.notes: Gulf-facing beach; open southern horizon. Public island, free access.

spots.name: Sipsey Wilderness, Bankhead NF
spots.notes: Remote national forest; reasonably dark for the Southeast.

spots.name: Sleeping Giant State Park
spots.notes: Ridge views; limited darkness due to surrounding suburbs. Best for sunsets.

spots.name: Cape Henlopen State Park
spots.notes: Atlantic-facing beach; open eastern horizon. Entry fee applies.

spots.name: Garden of the Gods, Shawnee NF
spots.notes: Southern Illinois; one of the darker spots in the state. Open sandstone overlooks.

spots.name: Hoosier National Forest (Tell City)
spots.notes: Southern Indiana; darker than the rest of the state.

spots.name: Effigy Mounds National Monument
spots.notes: Bluff overlooks above the Mississippi; reasonably dark rural area.

spots.name: Mammoth Cave National Park
spots.notes: Rural central Kentucky; open meadows around the visitor area are reasonably dark.

spots.name: Kisatchie National Forest
spots.notes: Central Louisiana; one of the few darker areas in the state.

spots.name: Monomoy National Wildlife Refuge, Cape Cod
spots.notes: Remote tip of Cape Cod; ocean horizon to the east and south. Access via ferry or kayak.

spots.name: Quabbin Reservoir Area
spots.notes: Central Massachusetts reservoir; one of the darker spots in the state. General area — explore public access points.

spots.name: Ozark National Scenic Riverways
spots.notes: Rural southern Missouri; open river valley with dark skies.

spots.name: Nebraska Sandhills (Valentine area)
spots.notes: One of the darkest regions in the continental US. Vast open grassland with 360-degree horizons. General area — use public roads and wildlife refuges.

spots.name: Adirondack Park (Blue Mountain Lake)
spots.notes: Large wilderness park; look for public boat launches and campgrounds for open views.

spots.name: Catskill Mountains (Slide Mountain area)
spots.notes: Closer to NYC but reasonably dark in the higher elevations.

spots.name: Blue Ridge Parkway (NC — Waterrock Knob)
spots.notes: High-elevation pullout on the Parkway; open western and southern views.

spots.name: Nantahala National Forest
spots.notes: Remote western NC mountains; general area is dark and accessible from forest roads.

spots.name: Hocking Hills State Park
spots.notes: One of Ohios darker accessible spots; wooded but clearings available.

spots.name: Wichita Mountains Wildlife Refuge
spots.notes: Open prairie refuge; surprisingly dark for Oklahoma. Free access.

spots.name: Block Island
spots.notes: Island off the coast; darker than the mainland. Ferry access required.

spots.name: Governor Dodge State Park
spots.notes: Southwest Wisconsin; one of the darker state parks. Open meadow areas.

spots.name: Apostle Islands National Lakeshore
spots.notes: Remote Lake Superior islands; very dark. Boat or kayak access required for islands.

spots.name: Valley of Fire State Park
spots.notes: Red sandstone formations; good sunset views and reasonably dark.

spots.name: Pinelands National Reserve (Pine Barrens)
spots.notes: Largest dark sky area in the NJ/NY metro region. General area — many public access roads through the forest.

spots.name: Grayson Highlands State Park
spots.notes: High-elevation SW Virginia; open balds with wide sky views.

spots.name: Elk County Dark Sky Area
spots.notes: North-central PA; second darkest region in the state after Cherry Springs.

spots.name: Big South Fork NRRA
spots.notes: Remote gorge area on the KY/TN border; open plateau sections are reasonably dark.

spots.name: Spruce Knob
spots.notes: Highest point in WV; very dark and open. Summit meadow has wide sky views.

spots.name: Deep Creek Lake area
spots.notes: Western Maryland mountains; one of the darker areas in the state.

spots.name: Cohutta Wilderness, Chattahoochee NF
spots.notes: Remote north Georgia mountains; general wilderness area, very dark.
